question: After submitting Stellar Manage Buy/Sell offers, how do I track offer ids, fills, cancellations, and path-payment executions through the SDEX orderbook?

keyFacts: Explains create/update/delete parameters and parses transaction result XDR/meta for the authoritative lifecycle transition and claimed offers.
Separates current open-offer state from trade history, including immediate fills and synthetic trade IDs.
Handles path-payment consumption, cursor pagination/reconnects, and explicitly states Classic offers do not expire.
avoid: Do NOT rely on unused Horizon offer-created/updated/removed effects to recover offer IDs.
Do NOT infer a missing offer was necessarily fully filled; distinguish result/meta, deletion/cancellation, current state, and trade history.
Do NOT invent protocol-level offer expiry or ignore path-payment executions.
golden.notes:
CORRECTED 2026-07-10: the inherited effects-based bot flow was not verified. Current source/live behavior requires result XDR/meta plus current offers and trade history; offer create/update/remove effects are unused.
Also good if the answer explains claimed offers, immediate fills with synthetic trade IDs, current-state/history separation, and path-payment correlation.
Golden cites: Stellar operation docs and Horizon/API docs for offers/effects/trades.
GT-17 CORRECTION 2026-07-10: result XDR/meta is authoritative; current offer state and trade history are separate; Classic offers have no protocol expiry.

corroboration.claim: Offer create/update/delete transitions are authoritative in transaction result/meta; current Horizon offer-created/updated/removed effects are unused.

evidence.ref: A: current Stellar XDR, SDEX, Horizon offer/trade, and state-archival documentation
evidence.note: verbatim legacy evidence descriptor imported at migration

evidence.ref: B: stellar-core offer operation source and stellar/go effect/TOID source mark offer effects unused and explain synthetic IDs
evidence.note: verbatim legacy evidence descriptor imported at migration

evidence.ref: F: a live successful offer update returned zero effects while decoded TransactionResult carried the updated OfferEntry; a removed offer 404ed while its offer-trades history remained
evidence.note: verbatim legacy evidence descriptor imported at migration

corroboration.claim: Classic SDEX offers expire at the protocol level.

evidence.ref: A: current validator docs state Classic ledger entries including offers do not expire
evidence.note: verbatim legacy evidence descriptor imported at migration

evidence.ref: B: OfferEntry XDR has no expiry/timestamp field
evidence.note: verbatim legacy evidence descriptor imported at migration

verified.by: Golden-truth sweep import — The inherited bot flow invented offer expiry and relied on Horizon offer-created/updated/removed effects that current stellar/go marks unused. Independent source and live-operation checks show lifecycle transitions in transaction result XDR/meta, current offer state, and trade history; immediate fills may use synthetic trade IDs.
evidence: Solo scratchpad 575 GT-17 primary process 3247 and blind process 3248, including live operation 272376861589106689 and offer/trade reads asOf 2026-07-10.
Sibling sweep: q-asset-path-payment-ops, q-asset-usdc-eurc-path-fx, q-defi-arbitrage-pathpayment-bots, q-asset-sdex-vs-amm, and q-defi-market-making-kelp.
rootCause: Stale documentation and generic API-absence caveats were promoted over current implementation behavior and invented protocol expiry.
eval-side lifecycle authoring lacked an empirical transaction/result/effects check
